name: SpeedDial
summary: Floating action button with slide-out action animation

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon for the main FAB (usually a ➕).

props.name: actions
props.description: Speed-dial actions revealed when the FAB is toggled.

props.name: direction
props.description: Direction in which actions should expand.

props.name: sx
props.description: Inline styles via CSSProperties (with CSS var support)

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Use sparingly. SpeedDial is for a small cluster of high‑value actions (typically 2–5). If you have more, consider a toolbar or menu instead.
Place with intent. Default bottom‑right works well; adjust offsets with spacing tokens to avoid overlapping content or platform UI (e.g., safe areas).

Label actions clearly. Provide short, specific labels; each action's `label` becomes its accessible name (`aria-label`) and hover `title`. The dial uses disclosure semantics (`aria-expanded`/`aria-controls` on the trigger, a `role='group'` actions container) and shows a visible keyboard focus ring.

Dismissal is automatic. The open dial registers on the shared overlay stack: pressing Escape closes it and returns focus to the FAB, clicking outside closes it, and invoking any action collapses the dial back to a stable state. You do not need to manage open/closed state yourself.
Pick direction by context. Choose `up` / `left` when anchored to bottom/right edges to prevent overflow. Keep action order predictable.
Motion tokens. Animations use theme motion tokens; keep custom presets aligned for a cohesive feel with the rest of the app.
